title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware variant specifically designed to target cloud-based infrastructure. The campaign uses compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure poses a significant threat by exploiting compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code. This can lead to widespread data encryption, service disruptions, financial losses, and potential data exfiltration.
recommended_actions: Implement strict access controls and regularly audit permissions for CI/CD pipelines to prevent unauthorized access.
Deploy endpoint detection and response (EDR) solutions and monitor for anomalous activities within cloud environments.
Ensure regular backups of critical data are performed and stored offline to facilitate recovery in case of a ransomware attack.

title: Show HN: Open-source vulnerability scanner
description: New tool automatically scans dependencies for known vulnerabilities and suggests patches. Gaining traction in the security community.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity.

The project was informed of the problem early through an issue report but has not responded ye

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The identified vulnerability in the Invoice Note Handler of Crater up to version 6.0.6 allows for cross-site scripting (XSS) attacks. This could enable attackers to inject malicious scripts into the application, potentially compromising user data, session tokens, or other sensitive information.
recommended_actions: Update Crater to a patched version as soon as it becomes available or apply any available workarounds to sanitize the 'notes' input.
Implement a web application firewall (WAF) to detect and block malicious input targeting this vulnerability.
Conduct a security audit of the application to identify and address other potential vulnerabilities.

title: Mojo::JSON versions before 9.47 for Perl allow memory exhaustion via unbounded recursion in the...
description:
Mojo::JSON versions before 9.47 for Perl allow memory exhaustion via unbounded recursion in the pure-Perl decoder.

The pure-Perl decode path (`_decode_value` dispatching to `_decode_array` and `_decode_object`) recurses with no depth limit, so a small deeply nested JSON document can consume excessive memory.

This path is the default when Cpanel::JSON::XS is not installed or `MOJO_NO_JSON_XS=1` is set; the Cpanel::JSON::XS fast path is not affected.

Any caller that decodes an untrusted JSON bo

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-14803.

title: A vulnerability was detected in radareorg radare2 up to 6.1.6. Affected by this issue is some...
description: A vulnerability was detected in radareorg radare2 up to 6.1.6. Affected by this issue is some unknown functionality of the file libr/bin/format/mdmp/mdmp.c of the component Memory64ListStream Parser. Performing a manipulation results in stack-based buffer overflow. The attack requires a local approach. The exploit is now public and may be used. The patch is named 175d4addb68981331c85b10681c2161c38fb5762. It is suggested to install a patch to address this issue.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in radare2's Memory64ListStream Parser could lead to stack-based buffer overflow, potentially allowing attackers to execute arbitrary code or crash the application. However, the attack requires local access, reducing its overall risk.
recommended_actions: Immediately apply the patch 175d4addb68981331c85b10681c2161c38fb5762 to mitigate the vulnerability.
Restrict local access to systems running radare2 to trusted users only.
Monitor systems for unusual behavior or crashes that may indicate exploitation attempts.

title: A security vulnerability has been detected in radareorg radare2 up to 6.1.6. Affected by this...
description: A security vulnerability has been detected in radareorg radare2 up to 6.1.6. Affected by this vulnerability is the function r_core_bin_load of the file libr/core/cfile.c. Such manipulation leads to use after free. The attack needs to be performed locally. The exploit has been disclosed publicly and may be used. The name of the patch is 635ab1eeb30340c26076722a90cb91fb2272130b. Applying a patch is advised to resolve this issue.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows local attackers to exploit a use-after-free issue in the r_core_bin_load function, potentially leading to application crashes or arbitrary code execution. However, the attack requires local access, limiting its scope.
recommended_actions: Apply the patch 635ab1eeb30340c26076722a90cb91fb2272130b to mitigate the vulnerability.
Ensure all systems using radare2 are updated to version 6.1.7 or later.
Limit local access to systems running radare2 to trusted users only.

title: A vulnerability has been found in SourceCodester Class and Exam Timetabling System 1.0/1.php. The...
description: A vulnerability has been found in SourceCodester Class and Exam Timetabling System 1.0/1.php. The impacted element is an unknown function of the file /edit_course1.php. The manipulation of the argument ID leads to sql injection. The attack can be initiated remotely. The exploit has been disclosed to the public and may be used.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows remote attackers to perform SQL injection, potentially leading to unauthorized access to sensitive data, database manipulation, or full system compromise. Public disclosure increases the likelihood of exploitation.
recommended_actions: Immediately apply input validation and parameterized queries to mitigate SQL injection vulnerabilities.
Update to a patched version of the software if available, or contact the vendor for support.
Monitor logs for unusual database activity and implement a web application firewall (WAF) to detect and block malicious requests.

title: A weakness has been identified in SourceCodester Onlne Examination & Learning Management System 1...
description: A weakness has been identified in SourceCodester Onlne Examination & Learning Management System 1.0. Affected by this issue is some unknown functionality of the file /announcements.php. Executing a manipulation can lead to unrestricted upload. The attack can be executed remotely. The exploit has been made available to the public and could be used for attacks. The name of the affected product appears to have a typo in it.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The identified vulnerability in SourceCodester Online Examination & Learning Management System 1.0 allows for unrestricted file uploads via /announcements.php. This could enable attackers to upload malicious files, potentially leading to remote code execution, data breaches, or system compromise. The public availability of the exploit increases the likelihood of attacks.
recommended_actions: Immediately apply input validation and file type restrictions on the /announcements.php endpoint to prevent malicious file uploads.
Update or patch the application to a secure version if available, or contact the vendor for support.
Monitor server logs and network traffic for signs of exploitation, and implement a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to block malicious requests.

title: CVE-2026-13523 - A weakness has been identified in GPAC up to 26.02.0. This affects an unknown part of the file src/u
description: A weakness has been identified in GPAC up to 26.02.0. This affects an unknown part of the file src/utils/base_encoding.c of the component ISOBMFF Parser. Executing a manipulation can lead to highly compressed data. The attack needs to be launched locally. The exploit has been made available to the public and could be used for attacks. This patch is called 297f2d8d1f493d8b241330533cd47f7da758aeb3. A patch should be applied to remediate this issue. The vendor confirms: "We added a check on inflate output size, if it surpasses 32 times the input size we stop in error. This value could be adjusted later."

title: CVE-2026-13514 - A weakness has been identified in Chess Play and Learn App up to 4.9.42 on Android. This issue affec
description: A weakness has been identified in Chess Play and Learn App up to 4.9.42 on Android. This issue affects some unknown processing of the file AndroidManifest.xml of the component com.chess. This manipulation causes exposure of backup file to an unauthorized control sphere. It is feasible to perform the attack on the physical device. The exploit has been made available to the public and could be used for attacks. Upgrading the affected component is advised. The vendor was informed early about this issue. They confirmed the existence and that they will address it. Furthermore, they explain that their bug bounty "explicitly excludes physical-access attacks". However, they appreciate the quality of the report and aim at making a goodwill payment to the researcher.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity. CVE: CVE-2026-13514.

It is stated that the exploitability is difficult. The exploit has been released to the public and may be used for attacks. The pull request to fix this issue awaits acceptance.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-13513.
